title: Sedgewick’s Algorithms (w/ visual aids)🕰️ Past–Present
content:
Sedgewick’s Algorithms (w/ visual aids)🕰️ Past–Present
Visual/nested systems, branching choices
Combines math with design clarity, helpful when building “readable recursion” in UI/UX layer
🧰 Suggested Workflow
Experimental / Mythic layer modeling / OS recursion shell designBuild tiny live simulations or metaphors of your logic before scaling.II. ⚙️ Operating Systems & Systems Design
📚 Technical Sources
Title
Temporal Mode
Use in System
Why It Matters
Modern Operating Systems (Tanenbaum)🕰️ Past–Present
Canonical base of your OS
OS = memory, processes, I/O, scheduling — you need this layer beneath the myth
Linux From Scratch🕰️ Present
OS Prototype Builder
If you ever want RE:GE_OS to boot from hardware or serve as a standalone shell, this will ground that fantasy
Systems Performance (Brendan Gregg)🕰️ Future
Optimization / Symbolic Diagnostics
Understand system bottlenecks; even metaphorically, informs symbolic “heatmaps,” signal flow, energy models
